instruction: What is Hive Engine and how does it work?
must_not_contain: Hive Engine is part of the Hive base layer
rationale: Hive Engine is a SEPARATE sidechain, not part of the Hive base layer. It has its own consensus and token system. HE tokens are NOT HIVE — they're sidechain assets that can be deposited/withdrawn via the HE gateway.

instruction: How do you create a custom token on Hive?
rationale: Custom tokens on Hive are created through Hive Engine (layer 2), not on the base layer. There is no native token creation mechanism on Hive L1. Referencing ERC-20 conflates Hive with Ethereum.

instruction: What is VSC (Virtual Smart Chain) on Hive?
rationale: VSC is a Layer 2 smart contract platform being built on Hive. As of 2026, it is still in development/testnet. It does NOT replace Hive Engine — they serve different purposes. It does NOT add smart contracts to Hive L1.

instruction: What are Hive communities and how do they work technically?
rationale: Hive communities are on-chain structures managed via custom_json operations with role-based permissions (owner, admin, mod, member). They are NOT smart contracts or separate chains. Calling them subreddits oversimplifies the on-chain governance model.

instruction: How does HivePoA (Proof of Access) work for decentralized storage?
must_not_contain: HivePoA is the same as IPFS
HivePoA stores data on the Hive blockchain
rationale: HivePoA is a decentralized storage network that uses Web of Trust for node reputation and content-addressed storage (CIDs). It does NOT store data on the Hive blockchain itself. It's a separate P2P network, not IPFS or Filecoin — though it shares concepts with content-addressed systems.
